# Copilot review instructions ‚Äî @kud/glyphs

## Project context

`@kud/glyphs` is a multi-language source of truth for terminal glyphs ‚Äî Nerd
Font codepoints, plain-unicode fallbacks, and emoji ‚Äî published as a
TypeScript ESM package and a standalone zsh plugin.

- **Toolchain**: TypeScript (strict), ESM only, built with `tsup`, tested with
  `vitest`, exact-pinned dependencies.
- **Pipeline**: `glyphs.json` (hand-authored `name ‚Üí { nerd?, unicode?,
emoji? }`) ‚Üí `scripts/generate.mjs` (`npm run generate`) ‚Üí `src/generated.ts`
  (typed TS consts) + `glyphs.plugin.zsh` (ready-to-source zsh plugin). `npm
run build` runs `generate` then `tsup`.
- **Public surface**: `src/index.ts` exports `glyphs`, `glyph(name, variant)`,
  and `renderZsh(options)` (`src/render.ts`). The library names no specific
  consumer ‚Äî `renderZsh()` takes a caller-supplied variable prefix (default
  `ICON`; shui uses `SHUI_ICON`, other consumers use their own).
- **Core hard rule**: Private-Use-Area codepoints (Nerd Font glyphs) are
  invisible in editors, diffs, and AI tooling, and must never appear as raw
  bytes in source. Every codepoint is emitted as an escape sequence by
  construction ‚Äî `\u{XXXX}` in TypeScript, `$'\U0000XXXX'` in zsh ‚Äî never
  pasted or typed as a literal glyph character.

## Content rules

Flag the following when seen in a diff:

- **Hand-edits to generated files.** `src/generated.ts` and
  `glyphs.plugin.zsh` carry a "Generated by scripts/generate.mjs ... do not
  edit by hand" banner. Any diff touching either file directly (rather than
  via `npm run generate` after a `glyphs.json` change) is a correctness bug ‚Äî
  the hand-edit will be silently overwritten and the two files will drift from
  `glyphs.json`.
- **`glyphs.json` changes without regeneration.** If `glyphs.json` changes but
  `src/generated.ts` / `glyphs.plugin.zsh` don't change in the same diff (or
  vice versa), flag it ‚Äî they must move together.
- **Raw PUA bytes.** Any literal Private-Use-Area character typed directly
  into source (TS, zsh, JSON, or scripts) instead of an escape sequence
  (`\u{XXXX}` / `$'\U0000XXXX'`) or a codepoint-hex string in `glyphs.json`.
  This is the project's hard rule ‚Äî treat it as blocking, not a nitpick.
- **Consumer-specific naming baked into the library.** `renderZsh()` and
  `glyphs.plugin.zsh` are consumer-agnostic by design (prefix is a parameter,
  default `ICON`). Flag any change that hardcodes a specific consumer's prefix
  (e.g. `SHUI_ICON`) or assumption into `src/` or `scripts/generate.mjs`
  itself, rather than leaving it as a caller-supplied option.
- **Non-exact dependency versions.** Any `^` or `~` range added to
  `dependencies`/`devDependencies` in `package.json` ‚Äî this repo pins exact
  versions only.
- **CommonJS or non-ESM patterns.** `require()`, `module.exports`, or `.mjs`
  extensions outside the existing `scripts/generate.mjs` convention ‚Äî the
  package is `"type": "module"`, ESM (`import`/`export`) throughout.
- **`function` declarations or classes.** New code should use `const fn = ()
=> {}` arrow functions; flag `function` keyword declarations or `class`
  usage in `src/` or `scripts/`.
- **Truncated or single-scalar assumptions about emoji.** Emoji values in
  `glyphs.json` may be multi-scalar grapheme clusters (e.g. `‚öÝÔ∏è` is two
  codepoints, `U+26A0 U+FE0F`). Flag any code that assumes one codepoint per
  glyph, slices a string by a fixed length, or drops trailing variation
  selectors/joiners when handling the `emoji` variant.
- **Missing `nerd` variant.** Every `glyphs.json` entry must have at least a
  `nerd` value (enforced by `src/index.test.ts`'s "more than 80 glyphs, each
  with a nerd variant" check) ‚Äî flag a new entry that omits it.
- **Untyped/loose lookups bypassing `GlyphName`/`Variant`.** Flag string
  literals used where the generated `GlyphName` or `Variant` union type would
  catch a typo at compile time (e.g. `glyphs["some-typo"]` cast through `any`
  instead of going through `glyph()`).
- **Test assertions on invisible glyphs by eye.** Tests must assert codepoints
  numerically (see the `cps()` helper in `src/index.test.ts`), never compare
  raw PUA characters as strings in a way a reviewer or CI log can't verify.

## Suppression rules

Stay quiet about:

- **Mirrored escape logic across `render.ts`, `scripts/generate.mjs`, and
  `src/index.test.ts`.** `zshEscape`/`tsEscape` are deliberately reimplemented
  in the test file (`glyphToZsh`) as an independent check, and separately in
  the build-time generator vs. the runtime `renderZsh()`. This is intentional
  cross-verification, not a DRY violation ‚Äî don't suggest extracting a shared
  helper across the runtime/build/test boundary.
- **Missing `unicode` fallback.** Not every glyph needs a `unicode` entry in
  `glyphs.json` ‚Äî only `nerd` is required. Don't flag an entry for lacking
  `unicode` or `emoji` unless `nerd` is also missing.
- **Verbose escape/codepoint-mapping comments in `scripts/generate.mjs`.**
  The file explains the PUA-invisibility problem at some length by design,
  since the whole point of the project is that these codepoints can't be
  eyeballed. Don't suggest trimming this context.
- **`dist/` and other generated/ignored output.** Never review anything under
  `dist/` (gitignored, tsup output) as if it were hand-written source.
- **Test-only duplication for independent verification** (e.g. `glyphToZsh` in
  `src/index.test.ts` mirroring `render.ts`'s `zshEscape`) ‚Äî see above, this is
  the point, not an oversight.

## Review format

Every comment must carry a risk label, explain why it matters in _this_
codebase, and propose a concrete fix ‚Äî never just name the problem.

**Risk labels (required on every comment):**

```
üî• blocking:  ‚Äî must fix before merge (correctness, security, data integrity)
üå∂Ô∏è concern:   ‚Äî should fix, not a blocker (reliability, maintainability)
üßä nitpick:   ‚Äî optional (style, naming, minor improvement)
```

**Comment structure (required):**

```
<risk-label>: <one-line summary of the issue>

<motivation sentence ‚Äî why this matters in this codebase specifically, not
just generically>

<concrete suggestion ‚Äî what to do, not just what's wrong>
```

Example:

```
üî• blocking: src/generated.ts edited by hand

This file is regenerated from glyphs.json by `npm run generate` and carries a
"do not edit by hand" banner ‚Äî a direct edit here will silently disappear the
next time anyone regenerates, and the source of truth (glyphs.json) won't
reflect this change at all.

Move this change into glyphs.json instead, then run `npm run generate` and
commit both the JSON and the regenerated output together.
```
